notes: famous cobble/brick lane; model scores brick as 0.70 flat regardless of how uneven it is

route start (snapped node 7633892893). Google Maps routes down Charles St and continues onto Mount Vernon St — larger roads that the current algorithm avoids. But the sidewalk width felt quite narrow compared to the Maps route, so the algorithm's avoidance may be reasonable here.

route end near the State House (snapped node 7721979137). Crossing intersections should be left up to the user: when an intersection requires both crossings, the user decides which to take first based on whichever light turns green first. In the route view, a double-crossing should minimize turns relative to the surrounding route to keep the path clean.

Roxbury->Franklin Park survey route. Path is sensible (comparable to Google Maps walking). Walkable in segments but the first half environment is unpleasant/busy. Arterial stretches have SIDEWALK GAPS (coverage issue). RESTRICTED_ACCESS=6 edges are genuinely restricted, but they sit at the route END: anyone walking the full route to that destination is a customer, so terminal customers/destination access should NOT be penalized like a mid-route private shortcut. Design idea: skip the restricted penalty on terminal edges of a route.

Beacon Hill->Downtown Crossing survey route. STEPS edge is a REAL staircase. Whether routing over it is acceptable DEPENDS ON USER -> needs an accessibility (step-free) toggle; fine for able-bodied, blocking for wheelchair/stroller. Path mostly sensible but has a few MORE TURNS than desired: the detours pick narrower, more-walkable streets, but extra turns hurt simplicity and the narrow streets could feel less safe. Future decisions: (1) accessibility/step-free mode, (2) turn-count/simplicity penalty, (3) a safety dimension distinct from walkability.

South End->Back Bay survey route. ~13 crossings are mostly LINEAR/reasonable (not zigzag); a wider path could avoid a few. Some real-world crossings are MISSING from OpenStreetMap (data gap -> undercounts true crossings). Major crossings (Mass Ave) are signalized. Path looks slightly odd vs Google Maps because many segments are car-unfit or illegal for cars so Maps won't route them; but on Street View the route feels SAFER and MORE walkable than Google's path. Positive validation that the walkability routing is working as intended.

Nubian Sq->Mass Ave survey route. Route uses QUIET streets, and even the bit of Washington St (arterial) it touches is a calm stretch -> model successfully avoided the busy arterial. Surface: some cracks in the first ~20%, good otherwise. Subjective walkability ~3 for the first 20%, ~4 for the rest. Path is sensible. Positive case.

Newmarket->Andrew survey route. Model OVERRATES (0.86 but feels ~3/medium). Root causes per inspection: (1) start is unpleasant/industrial but highway_score=0.55 (residential) does not capture the environment; (2) pristine concrete yields a near-1.0 surface_score, inflating the composite; (3) low-walk edges need MORE weight in cost (higher alpha / steeper penalty). KEY WEIGHTS BUG: surface_quality(2)+surface_material(2)=4 > road_type(3), so surface collectively outweighs highway_score -- contradicts the intended 'highway_score highest'. Fix idea: raise road_type weight above the surface sum. Sidewalk: continuous on Southampton, MISSING before it (coverage gap). Path itself sensible.

City Sq->Sullivan survey route. Model AVOIDS Rutherford Ave entirely -> a genuinely good pedestrian path exists and was found. Subjective ~4 along Bunker Hill St, drops to ~2 for the last ~100m. Slight overrating is only at the END and reflects the DESTINATION's nature, not the route. RECURRING THEME (see dudley): endpoint unpleasantness/restriction is destination-driven and should not count against the route's score. Path sensible.

North End->MGH/West End survey route. Greenway crossing is CLEAN (sensible signalized crossings). Brick scored slightly HIGHER than actual quality but brick is not very present here, so minor. Subjective ~4. Path mostly good BUT includes one UNNECESSARY crossing across Cambridge St -> supports a crossing/turn-minimization objective (see beacon_hill_to_downtown and the double-crossing note).

Copley->Fenway survey route. Average 0.92 is FAIR overall, but overrated due to specific CROSSING CHOICES (odd zigzag). Routes along busy Boylston instead of the nicer PARALLEL options (Newbury St / Comm Ave Mall tree-lined pedestrian mall) -> model has no greenery/amenity factor to prefer the pleasant parallel. Two takeaways: (1) crossing-minimization (recurring), (2) an amenity/greenery factor would let it choose the nicer equal-length parallel.

South Boston->Seaport survey route. Score 0.90 ABOUT RIGHT; 12 crossings reasonable for the big Seaport blocks (not zigzag); path sensible. Positive case. Useful CONTRAST with newmarket: pristine wide sidewalks are justified here because the environment is acceptable, so the surface-driven high score is correct -> confirms newmarket's overrating was the unpleasant ENVIRONMENT not being captured, not surface scoring itself.

Jamaica Plain / Centre St survey route. Feels SLIGHTLY worse than 0.87 (mild overrating); mostly residential, more public areas in the second half. Uses quiet BACK STREETS rather than the lively Centre St main drag -> fine for a quiet preference, but mirrors copley_to_fenway in reverse: no vibrancy/amenity factor to weigh a lively shop-lined street vs quiet streets (could be a user preference toggle). Path sensible.
model 70, feels ~80. eyes value too low. routing takes wrong side w/ extra crossing over West Cedar; seg1 misses West Cedar crossing.
model 54, feels ~90. SAFETY too low: arterial=0.09 from nearby secondary roadway crushes it despite eyes 0.95. Newbury/Comm traffic is slow -> wants traffic-speed nuance.
model 81, feels ~85. env=0.30 on Paul Revere FOOTPATH makes no sense (protected pedestrian way penalized for nearby waterfront road).
model 58, feels ~80. env on segs 2,3,4,5 all too low for a residential+shops block (the ideal case).

model 34, about right. PATH/location bug: origin snapped onto the primary adjacent to the footway -> forced along primary until an intersection U-turn (segs 1,3). DATA: segs 5,6 surf=0.03/0.00 but same surface as 4,7,8,9 -> bad SCI/match.
model 48, feels ~65. PATH too low: highway=residential (0.55) undersells these sidewalks; tag on segs 2,3,5,6 feels inappropriate vs reality.

model 43, path effectively UNWALKABLE: seg5 closed to public; segs 6,7,8 not in street map. Crossings less bad than flagged (high count = refuge-island splits of big roads). Beginning section makes 43 feel slightly low.
model 40, feels ~80. SAFETY/env consistently too low: wide sidewalk + many pedestrians + regulated crossings. Crossings (not env) should be the lowering factor. surf=0.69 segs2-5 slightly low.
model 59, feels ~75. env too low again. routing takes wrong side of Washington (extra crossing seg3) and Spring Lane (feels like a back alley) over Water St.
model 67, feels ~75. env too low again (residential main street).
